requirements.target: to be able to spin up multiple replicas of this validator just by changing the replica count in the compose file – nothing fancy

rules.description: in the compose file – nothing fancy

requirements.source: We'll be orchestrating everything with Docker Compose, and the customer (well, actually our own product teams) needs to be able to spin up multiple replicas of this validator just by changing the replica count in the compose file – nothing fancy, just standard Docker Compose scaling.

requirements.target: to be observable the way the rest of the platform already is
requirements.source: Day-to-day operations need to be observable the way the rest of the platform already is.

rules.description: so we can collect them with the rest of the logs )

requirements.source: All logs have to go to a single file at /var/log/confluence-validator.log inside the container (we'll bind-mount that to the host so we can collect them with the rest of the logs).

rules.description:
so the downstream services know precisely where the change occurred , 
 - if the page was heavily rewritten ( more than half the content changed ) it can just return the whole new version

requirements.source:
so the downstream services know precisely where the change occurred,
- if the page was heavily rewritten (more than half the content changed) it can just return the whole new version – we don't want to over-engineer the diff logic.

rules.description: with the Python / FastAPI / Docker pattern

requirements.source: We need something robust, boringly reliable, and completely in line with the Python/FastAPI/Docker pattern the rest of the platform already follows.
If you can deliver that, we'll be able to drop it into production next sprint with almost zero friction.
